Integration Fixture

Overview

This section describes the overall approach to implementing the feature and outlines the key decisions made during the design phase. The solution prioritises clarity and maintainability over raw performance.

Constraints

Several hard constraints govern the implementation. The artifact schema must remain backward-compatible with all existing consumers. No breaking changes may be introduced to the public API surface without a major version bump and an explicit migration guide.

Error Handling

All subagent dispatch calls must be wrapped in try/catch blocks. On failure the resolver records a structured error_enum value in the sidecar thread and continues to the next open thread rather than aborting the entire resolution run.

Clarification Protocol

When the subagent cannot determine the correct edit without additional operator guidance, it returns a clarification object. The resolver presents the question to the operator and re-dispatches with the chosen answer. The clarification cap is set to one round per thread to prevent infinite loops.

Conflict Scenario A

This section intentionally shares its document-offset range with the adjacent section to trigger the edit_conflicted error path in the resolver. The wave planner should segregate these into separate waves when possible.

Conflict Scenario B

This section is paired with conflict-a for overlap testing. Both sections are used together in the batch-mode conflict assertion to verify that the resolver handles the edit_conflicted error_enum correctly and does not crash the wave.

Idempotency Check

To clarify: the workflow steps and their ordering are described in full below. Each step must be executed in the prescribed sequence to ensure idempotent behaviour across repeated resolution runs. Steps that have already been applied are detected via semantic scoring and skipped.
